overview: The Vanguard Balanced ETF Portfolio (VBAL.TO) is an all-in-one asset allocation ETF that maintains a 60% equity / 40% fixed-income split. The equity portion is globally diversified across Canadian, U.S., international developed, and emerging market stocks through underlying Vanguard ETFs. The fixed-income sleeve is composed entirely of bonds — Canadian aggregate bonds, U.S. aggregate bonds, and global aggregate bonds via underlying Vanguard bond ETFs. There are NO REITs, real assets, or alternative investments in the fixed-income portion. VBAL automatically rebalances to maintain its 60/40 target, making it a true set-and-forget solution for moderate-risk investors. MER is 0.24%.
bullCase: All-in-one solution with automatic rebalancing — no need to manage multiple ETFs.
Low MER of 0.24% for a globally diversified balanced portfolio.
60/40 equity/bond split suits moderate-risk investors seeking both growth and stability.
bearCase: Rising interest rates hurt the 40% bond allocation, dragging overall returns.
Investors seeking aggressive growth may find the 60/40 split too conservative.
No ability to customize regional or sector weightings within the portfolio.
analystSummary: VBAL is the quintessential balanced ETF for Canadian investors who want a simple 60/40 portfolio. The fixed-income portion is pure bonds (Canadian, U.S., and global aggregate) — no REITs or alternatives. At 0.24% MER, it is hard to beat for hands-off investing.
seoDescription: Explore if VBAL is a good buy with our comprehensive analysis — Vanguard Balanced ETF Portfolio, 60/40 equity/bonds at 0.24% MER.
